summary: Tested canvas-based aurora background integration from GitHub. All 6 test cases PASSED. Aurora renders correctly on Login page and Dashboard with visible light curtains. No JavaScript errors. API endpoint returns 200. Login flow works.

features_tested.details: Returns {} as expected for initial state

features_tested.feature: Canvas aurora renders on Login page
Canvas aurora renders on Dashboard after login
features_tested.details: Aurora background visible behind topbar, project cards, prompt textarea

features_tested.feature: No JavaScript errors related to AuroraBackground or auroraEngine
Dashboard loads correctly with all UI elements visible
features_tested.details: Topbar, project grid, prompt textarea all visible
Login via Enter key submission works with REDACTED_LEAKED_USER / REDACTED_LEAKED_PASSWORD

critical_code_review_comments: AuroraBackground.jsx correctly imports auroraEngine and renders canvas with data-testid attributes for testing
Dashboard.jsx properly imports AuroraBackground at line 25 and renders it with activityLevel state (line 396)
LoginPage.jsx correctly imports and renders AuroraBackground at line 12 and 110
Aurora config API route properly registered in route dispatcher at line 23 and 60
activityLevel decay useEffect (lines 399-423) correctly spikes to 1 on message send and decays over ~6s
context_for_next_testing_agent: Canvas aurora background integration fully tested and working. Key findings: (1) Aurora renders on both Login and Dashboard pages with visible purple/turquoise light curtains. (2) Canvas has proper dimensions (1920x1080). (3) No JS errors. (4) API endpoint /api/aurora/config returns 200. (5) activityLevel prop wired correctly - spikes on chat, decays when idle. (6) Login requires Enter key (clicking Sign In button doesn't work - known minor UX issue from iteration_43).
